spec_match.rationale: The page uses `<permission type="camera microphone">` — the broader `<permission>` element from the PEPC explainer — rather than `<usermedia>` specifically. They are related elements, but this v149 feature ships `<usermedia>` specifically. The conflation muddies the spec match.

interactive.rationale: Two approach tabs (element vs JS), live `navigator.permissions.query()` reads, transition state machine, and graceful denied-state fallback. Real DOM, not a static card.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises the side-by-side comparison and live permission state — both render.

edge_cases.rationale: The denied path is handled and the recovery story is told, but the no-camera-device case (`NotFoundError`) and the `granted-then-permission-revoked-mid-session` paths are not exercised.

references.rationale: The chromestatus link in see-also points to `chromestatus.com/feature/5171071917416448` — that is the broader `<permission>` element feature, not the `<usermedia>` MVP this folder critiques. The correct ID for this folder is 4926233538330624.

design_system.rationale: Variables and tokens used throughout, but six instances of the unicode `→` arrow in section headers / event-flow text where the design rules say inline SVG, never glyphs.

openQuestions.title: Fix the chromestatus reference ID
openQuestions.detail: The see-also link reads `chromestatus.com/feature/5171071917416448` (the PEPC `<permission>` element). The correct ID for `Capability Elements <usermedia> MVP` is 4926233538330624. Update the link to keep the slug-recovery script honest.

openQuestions.title: Disambiguate `<permission>` vs `<usermedia>`
openQuestions.detail: The page exclusively uses `<permission type="camera microphone">` but the folder is the `<usermedia>` feature. Add either an explicit note `this concept showcases the broader permission element, which is the same family but not this specific feature`, or rewrite to use `<usermedia>` so the spec match is clean.

openQuestions.title: Replace `→` arrows with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: Six unicode arrows appear in the event flow chips and section headers. Convention is inline SVG with `currentColor` so dark mode and theming behave.

openQuestions.title: Demo mid-session permission revocation
openQuestions.detail: If the user opens chrome://settings and revokes camera permission while the stream is active, the track ends. Add a status panel that listens for `track.ended` and shows the element transitioning back to its inert state — a real-world UX trap.

openQuestions.title: Probe the NotFoundError branch
openQuestions.detail: Devices without a camera surface this synchronously via `navigator.mediaDevices.enumerateDevices()`. Add a probe that warns when no input is available before the user clicks.

summary: Useful side-by-side onboarding flow that nails the live permissions API, but it talks about `<permission>` while the folder is `<usermedia>` — and links to the wrong chromestatus ID. Fix the ID and disambiguate before anything else.
